q: A 65-year-old male presents with worsening shortness of breath and orthopnea. On examination, there are bilateral bibasilar crackles and 2+ pitting edema in the lower extremities. The BNP level is significantly elevated. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 72-year-old female presents with syncope on exertion. On auscultation, a harsh crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur is heard at the right upper sternal border, radiating to the carotids. The S2 heart sound is diminished. What is the likely valvular abnormality?
